description: Evaluates access management service maturity—access control systems, network and application access processes, identity and posture-based rules, remote access, isolation, access matrices, guest and OOB access, data access, custodianship, and SOC logging.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: An access management service exists and is reviewed frequently for efficacy.

capabilities.name: KPIs/KRIs
capabilities.description: Access management KPIs and KRIs are documented and reviewed frequently.

capabilities.name: Access Management System
capabilities.description: Centralized access control technology manages enforcement points with dynamic policy deployment and PDP configuration review.
evaluation_focus: Access control for all enforcement points
Centralized vs siloed policy decision points
PDP configuration review aligned to policies
Abstracted policy push to enforcement points

capabilities.name: Network Access Process
capabilities.description: Network and endpoint need-to-know access is provisioned via formalized processes with automation, consistent location scope, and frequent review.

capabilities.name: Application Access Process
capabilities.description: Application need-to-know access is provisioned via formalized processes with consistent scope and frequent review.
evaluation_focus: Consistent application access across locations and systems

capabilities.name: Identity based rules
capabilities.description: Identity-based access control is adopted and deployed at all enforcement points.
evaluation_focus: Identity-based rules at all enforcement points

capabilities.name: Access rules considering device posture
capabilities.description: Device posture is considered for network access from all locations.
evaluation_focus: Subject posture considered from all locations

capabilities.name: Asset Attributes
capabilities.description: Enterprise asset attributes are considered for application access for all subjects.
evaluation_focus: Asset attributes considered for all subjects

capabilities.name: Remote access across untrusted networks based on identity and device posture
capabilities.description: Remote access is formalized with consistent policies across gateways and advanced SSE or internet access without VPN.
evaluation_focus: Consistent remote access policy across gateways and LAN
Cloud SSE for internal asset access

capabilities.name: Access Isolation (Separation of duties) and its automation
capabilities.description: Production, development, and deployment access is separated with automated CI/CD removal of development access from production.
evaluation_focus: User separation across production, development, and deployment
Separate permissions for service and normal accounts
Automated CI/CD removal of dev access from production

capabilities.name: Access Matrix
capabilities.description: A granular subject-to-object access matrix applies enterprise-wide and is reviewed frequently.

capabilities.name: Guest Access
capabilities.description: Guest user onboarding is formalized with automated provisioning and deprovisioning.

capabilities.name: OOB device access
capabilities.description: Out-of-band access is formalized with secure protocols, consistent access control, and frequent review.
evaluation_focus: Consistent OOB access control across source locations

capabilities.name: Data access
capabilities.description: Data access is formalized with location-independent access and frequent review.

capabilities.name: Access Management Service custodian
capabilities.description: A separate access management service manager maintains separation of duties from authentication, credential management, and Active Directory.

capabilities.name: Access, authorization and accounting and logging
capabilities.description: Access logs, activity, and lifecycle changes are logged securely to the SOC, including dynamic changes.
evaluation_focus: Secure access activity logging to SOC

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
